methodology_sections: 1. Conservation Laws - Solvency, inflation checks, supply integrity
2. Relationship & Ratio Invariants - Collateralization, rate consistency
3. Economic Monotonicity - Yield accumulation, exchange rate direction
4. Incentive & Game Theoretic Integrity - Attack cost vs profit, slashing
5. Fee & Yield Consistency - Fee accounting, yield distribution

invariants.description: A user's userTotalStaked must always exactly equal the sum of their active self-stake amount and all their active community-stake amounts.
invariants.attack_scenario: If userTotalStaked is not increased during a slash release, subsequent withdrawals of the released amount will revert due to arithmetic underflow (userTotalStaked[msg.sender] -= amount), permanently locking the user's restored funds in the contract.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_userTotalStaked(address staker) public view { uint88 expected = selfStakes[staker].amount; /* + sum of community stakes */ assert(userTotalStaked[staker] == expected); }

invariants.description: The contract's GTC token balance must be greater than or equal to the sum of all active stake amounts plus the total slashed amounts pending burn.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the contract balance falls below its liabilities (active stakes + pending burns), users will be unable to withdraw their unlocked stakes, leading to a bank run and protocol insolvency.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_solvency() public view { assert(token.balanceOf(address(this)) >= totalActiveStakes + totalSlashed[currentSlashRound] + totalSlashed[currentSlashRound - 1]); }

invariants.description: The unlock time for any given stake can only be extended, never reduced.
invariants.attack_scenario: If an attacker can reduce the unlock time, they can bypass the mandatory 12-104 week lockup period, withdrawing funds prematurely to evade pending slashes for malicious behavior.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_unlockTimeMonotonicity(address staker) public { assert(selfStakes[staker].unlockTime >= previousUnlockTime[staker]); }

invariants.description: Funds can only be released from the current slash round or the immediately preceding slash round, as older rounds have already been burned.
invariants.attack_scenario: If a releaser can release funds from a round older than currentSlashRound - 1, they would be restoring balances that have already been permanently burned, creating unbacked liabilities and stealing from other users' deposits.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_release_round(uint16 slashRound) public view { assert(slashRound >= currentSlashRound - 1); }

invariants.description: The totalSlashed amount for a specific round must exactly equal the sum of all individual slashedAmounts assigned to that round.
invariants.attack_scenario: If totalSlashed is less than the sum of individual slashed amounts, lockAndBurn will burn fewer tokens than required. If greater, it will burn tokens belonging to active stakes, causing insolvency.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_totalSlashed(uint16 round) public view { assert(totalSlashed[round] == calculateSumOfSlashedInRound(round)); }
